quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: July 1st marks the halfway point of the year! It's also known as Canada Day, when our neighbors to the north celebrate with fireworks and festivals. Here in the Pacific Northwest, the summer season is in full swing with longer daylight hours—perfect for enjoying the beautiful Olympic Peninsula.

short_read.title: The Secret Lives of Harbor Seals in the Salish Sea
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals are common visitors to the Strait of Juan de Fuca, and they're much more fascinating than you might think. Learn about their playful personalities and surprising underwater abilities.

medium_read.title: Why Lavender Thrives in the Pacific Northwest
medium_read.teaser: The climate around Port Angeles is surprisingly similar to the lavender fields of Provence, France. Discover why this fragrant herb loves our region and how it attracts beneficial pollinators to gardens.

long_read.title: The Story of Olympic National Park: From Forest to Forever
long_read.teaser: Olympic National Park's creation in 1938 was the result of decades of passionate conservation efforts. This fascinating history reveals how local advocates fought to preserve the wilderness you can still enjoy today.

port_angeles.title: Butterfly Season at the Olympic Peninsula
port_angeles.description: July is peak butterfly season around Port Angeles! Watch for beautiful species like the Western Tiger Swallowtail and the Painted Lady in local gardens and parks. The warm summer weather brings these colorful visitors out in abundance, especially around wildflower meadows.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart can beat up to ___ times per minute while flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's exactly right, Barbara! Those tiny hearts work incredibly hard to power such amazing flight.
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1200 beats per minute—that's about 20 beats every second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: It's actually 1200 beats per minute! That's faster than we can even count. Their tiny hearts are remarkable little engines.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the fastest metabolism of any animal, which is why they need to eat almost constantly—visiting hundreds of flowers each day. When they sleep at night, their heart rate can drop to as low as 50 beats per minute to conserve energy.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is indeed the Hungarian word for butterfly—such a lovely, lyrical word!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The answer is 'pillangó'—such a beautiful word! Madár means bird, virág means flower, and napsugár means sunbeam.
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: The Hungarian word 'pillangó' is thought to come from the word 'pillant' meaning 'a glance' or 'a flutter,' perfectly capturing the quick, delicate movements of a butterfly's wings. Hungarian has many poetic words for nature that reflect the country's deep appreciation for the natural world.

puzzle_3.question: What special feature do Olympic Peninsula black bears have that helps them in the rainforest?
options: They hibernate longer than other bears
puzzle_3.correct_response: That's right! Olympic Peninsula black bears are excellent swimmers and regularly cross rivers and even swim in the Strait to move between areas. They're quite at home in this wet environment!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Actually, they're exceptional swimmers! Black bears on the Olympic Peninsula regularly swim across rivers and inlets, and have even been spotted swimming in the Strait of Juan de Fuca. It's one of the ways they adapted to the rainforest environment.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Black bears in the Olympic Peninsula live in one of the wettest temperate rainforests in North America, receiving over 12 feet of rain annually in some areas. Their swimming abilities help them access salmon streams and explore the network of waterways throughout their territory.
puzzle_3.learn_more_label: Olympic black bears — National Park Service

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
